summary: Pristine soundboard recording with a magical second set – Peggy‑O, Wang Dang Doodle, Bird Song and Stella Blue shine.
review: The show captured on gd91-10-28.sbd.vernon is widely praised for its emotional weight (Bill Graham’s funeral day) and superb playing, especially the soaring second‑set jams.  The soundboard source is mostly crystal‑clear, though a few reviewers note occasional drop‑outs and clicks in the first set.  Highlights repeatedly mentioned are Peggy‑O/El Paso, Wang Dang Doodle, Bird Song, Space and Stella Blue.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (≥10) give 4‑5 stars and describe the performance as superb; recording is generally praised as pristine with only minor glitches, yielding a strong combined score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Mostly crisp and pristine soundboard capture; a few comments about drop‑outs, clicks and pops in the first set but overall very listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Rough, somber opening (Bertha, Greatest Story Ever Told, Promised Land) followed by an emotionally charged second set that many describe as sublime and unifying.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s vocal delivery on Peggy‑O is highlighted as touching; his guitar work is generally praised as superb.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass is lauded as “flame‑breathed” and a driving force behind the second‑set intensity.
